rulings.Case Title: JENNY SAI-YUN SHU CHANG ET AL VS. SARA SHU CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT SARA CHAN, ESTHER SHU Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement is continued to August 26, 2022 pending arbitration. By no later than August 19, 2022 parties to file a joint declaration re: status of arbitration. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE VS. 166 GEARY STREET RETAIL OWNER LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion And Unopposed Motion For: (1) Appointment Of Receiver, And; (2) Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE Notice Of Unopposed Motion And Unopposed Motion For: (1) Appointment Of Receiver, And; (2) Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of Receiver is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service for the motion on file as of May 24, 2022. The POS is not executed by a person purported to have accomplished the service ("causing the documents to be placed in a sealed FEDEX envelope and the envelope to be delivered to Fedex delivery agent for delivery" and "I am readily familiar with the firm's practice of collection and processing correspondence for mailing. Under that practice it would be deposited with the U.S. Postal Service on that same day with postage thereon fully prepaid in the ordinary course of business.") No connection is established between firms practice for mailing via US Post Service and purported service in this case by "causing" the documents "to be delivered to FedEx agent for delivery." The person effectuating the service must state that the person provided the envelop to the FedEx agent for delivery or that the person is familiar with the firm's practice for mailing via FedEx. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Response To Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction, Supporting Declaration, And Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT MARK SMITH Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Response To Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction Hearing Required. Parties to appear in person at 8:30 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts regarding the service of the From Interrogatories, response to Demand for Production and the substance of the responses to Form Interrogatories. Parties to report their progress at 9:30 a.m. A sign-in sheet will be posted between 8:30 a.m. and 8:40 a.m. The Court will further address the request for $1,000 in sanctions; defendant is in pro per and attorney's fees are not available; however costs such as postage, copying fees, parking to attend the hearing, etc. will be considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
